scope_note: QA Koenig Shell Structure cert [293]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers Oxford ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5 Ch.XIII (Pell equation, norm form b^2-2e^2, representability by norms in Z[sqrt(2)]); Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (BEDA tuples, Koenig I=|C-F|). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For any (b,e) in Z>0 x Z>0 with I(b,e)=|b^2-2e^2|=k, the QA successor (b',e')=(b+2e,b+e) satisfies: (1) SHELL_PRESERVE: I(b',e')=k (shell preserved); (2) SIGN_FLIP: b'^2-2e'^2=-(b^2-2e^2) (sign alternates); (3) FAREY_K: |be'-b'e|=k (Farey det = I); (4) SPREAD_K: Wildberger spread between direction lines = k^2/(G_tilde*G_tilde') where G_tilde=b^2+e^2; (5) SHELL_UNIQUE: I=1 is the unique k with consecutive QA pairs Farey-adjacent (det=1). Empty shells: primes p with p≡±3(mod 8) are inert in Z[sqrt(2)] and do not appear as I values -- verified k=3,5,6,10,11 have no solutions for b,e<=100. Shell k is preserved under QA dynamics; its Farey det is the invariant k itself. Algebraic proof of FAREY_K: |b(b+e)-(b+2e)e|=|b^2-2e^2|=k. Algebraic proof of SIGN_FLIP: (b+2e)^2-2(b+e)^2=-(b^2-2e^2). Theorem NT: all integer and Fraction; no float state. Mechanism chain: cert [289] (Koenig Pell Ford Circle, k=1 case); cert [292] (Koenig Spread Optimality, SPREAD_DEV_K corollary); cert [141] (QA Pell Norm). Lineage: derived 2026-06-01.
